title: Mildly Fermented Chinese Chaozhou Fish Stew
description: Fatty and flavorful, this traditional Chaozhou fish stew is a low-carb-friendly dish that's perfect for special occasions. The banana leaf wrapping enhances the aroma and texture of the dish.

ingredients.name: Fish Stock
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, rich and flavorful

ingredients.name: Ferment-kimchi ( Chaozhou-style)
ingredients.notes: Spicy and sour, made with kimchi and fermented fish sauce

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Sliced thinly, adds warmth and depth to the stew

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced, adds aroma and flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Adds umami flavor and richness to the stew

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances the savory flavors of the dish

ingredients.name: White Pepper
ingredients.notes: Adds a subtle warmth and aroma to the stew

ingredients.name: Banana Leaves
ingredients.notes: Used for wrapping and steaming the stew

ingredients.name: Scallions
ingredients.notes: Used as garnish, adds freshness and crunch to the dish

instructions: Combine fish stock, ferment-kimchi, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sugar, and white pepper in a large pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes.
Add the fish to the pot and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare the banana leaves by cutting them into large sheets and blanching them in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Remove the leaves from the water and let them cool slightly.
Spoon the fish mixture onto the center of each leaf, then fold the leaves over the filling and secure with toothpicks or kitchen twine.
Steam the fish bundles over boiling water for 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the leaves are tender.
Serve the fish stew hot, garnished with scallions and additional ferment-kimchi if desired.
